chapter_context.summary: Hezekiah invites all Israel and Judah to celebrate a massive Passover; he prays for unpurified worshippers, extending the feast.

outline.text: Hezekiah Sends Letters Inviting All Israel to the Passover
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hezekiah Sends Letters Inviting All Israel to the Passover (30:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Passover Celebrated; Hezekiah Prays for Unclean Worshippers
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Passover Celebrated; Hezekiah Prays for Unclean Worshippers (30:13-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: The Feast Extended Seven More Days with Great Rejoicing
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Feast Extended Seven More Days with Great Rejoicing (30:21-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: The Temple Centered Kingdom
key_themes.description: The presence of God's glory in the Temple is the absolute center of national blessing and life.

key_themes.title: Immediate Retribution and Reform
key_themes.description: Good kings who seek the LORD experience immediate victory and peace, while unfaithfulness leads to immediate warning and exile.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 30 prompt you to humble yourself, seek God's face, and turn away from compromise?
What practical reforms can you make in your daily walk to ensure your heart is dedicated to God?

cultural_historical.title: The Levitical Worship System
cultural_historical.text: Unlike the Book of Kings, Chronicles highlights the roles of singers, Levites, and liturgical musicians in the temple's daily administration.
